---
title: "Skill Demand and Supply in Poland 2025"
subtitle: "Insights from Online Job Postings and Trainings"
date: today
---

## Executive Summary

::: {.hero}
```{=html}
<div class="hero-title">Executive Summary</div>
```

This report analyses labour demand and supply in Poland through the lens
of online job advertisements and training provision data. It combines
web-scraped vacancy data from <a class="paper-link" href="https://www.pracuj.pl/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Pracuj.pl</a>,
the largest online job portal in Poland, with information from the
<a class="paper-link" href="https://uslugirozwojowe.parp.gov.pl/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Baza Usług Rozwojowych (BUR)</a>,
the country's main database of training and upskilling services. The analysis
focuses on how employers signal required skills and how these relate to
the skills offered through the training system in 2025.

The study integrates labour market analysis with modern NLP methods,
including automated text processing, classification, and LLM-assisted
skill extraction, to transform unstructured job postings and training
descriptions into comparable measures of skill demand and skill supply.

Using mappings to two skill taxonomies, ESCO and Lightcast, the report
compares the composition of requested and supplied skills across
occupations, with particular attention to digital competencies and
emerging skill needs. It also introduces indicators of relative labour
demand, derived from job posting intensity, and contrasts them with the
structure of available training.

The Polish labour market is undergoing rapid transformation, with
increasing digitalization shaping both hiring practices and skill
development pathways. Online job postings provide a timely proxy for
employer demand, while training data from BUR offers insight into the
supply of skills and reskilling opportunities, enabling a joint
assessment of potential mismatches between demand and supply.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="kpi-grid" id="summary-cards"></div>
```

## Regional Coverage

::: {.section-card .map-shell}
### Relative labour demand

```{=html}
<div class="figure-caption-title" id="map-figure-title"></div>
```

The map shows regional demand and training density normalized by the
average labour force in 2025. Use the switch above the map to change the
view between job offers and BUR training services.

```{=html}
<div id="regional-map"></div>
<div class="map-caption source-note" id="map-note"></div>
```
:::

::: {.section-card}
### Regional comparison table

Counts are shown alongside their labour-force-normalized counterparts to
make regional demand and supply comparable across differently sized
voivodeships. Click a column header to sort the table.

```{=html}
<div class="data-table-wrap" id="regional-table"></div>
```
:::

::: {.section-card}
```{=html}
<div class="section-head">
  <div>
    <p class="section-kicker">Quick read</p>
    <h3>Key regional patterns</h3>
    <p id="highlights-copy"></p>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="rank-grid" id="highlights-grid"></div>
```
:::

## Data and Methodology

::: {.section-card}
### Data collection

We collect job advertisements from
<a class="paper-link" href="https://www.pracuj.pl/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Pracuj.pl</a>,
the largest online job portal in Poland, and training-service listings
from
<a class="paper-link" href="https://uslugirozwojowe.parp.gov.pl/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Baza Usług Rozwojowych</a>,
the country's main public database of training and upskilling services.
The analytical sample shown in this report focuses on records that can
be assigned to Polish voivodeships and linked to the regional demand and
supply comparison.

For job advertisements, we retain records with sufficient textual
content to support downstream NLP processing, including job titles and
descriptions. For BUR, we use training-service records with usable
regional metadata and service descriptions. We remove duplicates and
standardize core fields before the records enter the analytical
pipeline.

```{=html}
<p class="notes source-note" id="sources-note"></p>
```
:::

::: {.section-card}
### Processing pipeline

Raw job postings and training descriptions contain noisy, unstructured
information. We transform them into a standardized analytic dataset in
six steps: we first identify the source language, translate non-Polish
text into Polish, isolate skill-relevant sentences, map extracted skills
to the ESCO and Lightcast skill taxonomies, standardize job titles to
ESCO occupations using both titles and skill context for
disambiguation, and then enrich each record with regional information
and labour-force denominators for voivodeship-level comparison.

```{=html}
<div class="pipeline-diagram">
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">01</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#e0f2fe;color:#0369a1;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M6 4h9a3 3 0 0 1 3 3v6a3 3 0 0 1-3 3H9l-4 4v-4H6a3 3 0 0 1-3-3V7a3 3 0 0 1 3-3z"/><path d="M8 9h5"/><path d="M8 12h3"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Language<br>Detection</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Automatic identification of the source language for each job posting or training description</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">02</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#fef3c7;color:#b45309;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 8h6"/><path d="M8 6v2c0 2.1-1 4.2-2.8 5.6"/><path d="M6 12c.8 1.2 2 2.2 3.4 2.8"/><path d="m13 7 6 10"/><path d="m19 7-6 10"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Translation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Non-Polish descriptions are translated into Polish before downstream NLP processing</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">03</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#ccfbf1;color:#0d9488;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M4 6h16"/><path d="M7 12h10"/><path d="M10 18h4"/><circle cx="6.5" cy="6.5" r="1"/><circle cx="17.5" cy="12.5" r="1"/><circle cx="8.5" cy="18.5" r="1"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Skill-Sentence<br>Filtering</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Skill-relevant fragments are identified in vacancy and training text before structured extraction</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">04</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#ede9fe;color:#7c3aed;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="7"/><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="3"/><path d="M12 2v3"/><path d="M12 19v3"/><path d="M2 12h3"/><path d="M19 12h3"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Skill<br>Taxonomy Mapping</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Extracted skills are mapped to the ESCO and Lightcast taxonomies to make demand and supply directly comparable while retaining granular AI signals</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">05</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#dbeafe;color:#1d4ed8;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M7 4h10"/><path d="M9 8h6"/><path d="M12 8v9"/><path d="M8 20h8"/><path d="M5 12c1.2-1.4 2.8-2 4.7-2"/><path d="M19 12c-1.2-1.4-2.8-2-4.7-2"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Job Title<br>Standardisation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Job titles are mapped to ESCO occupations using titles and extracted skills as context for disambiguation</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="pipeline-step-top">
      <div class="pipeline-step-number">06</div>
      <div class="pipeline-icon" style="background:#dcfce7;color:#15803d;">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="30" height="30" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.9" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M4 7h16"/><path d="M4 12h10"/><path d="M4 17h7"/><path d="M17 11c2.2 0 4 1.8 4 4s-1.8 4-4 4-4-1.8-4-4"/><path d="m17 13 1.4 1.4L21 12"/></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pipeline-label">Data<br>Augmentation</div>
    <div class="pipeline-detail">Records are enriched with NACE sector information and geolocation used for regional assignment</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Our pipeline outperforms the current state of the art by approximately
3 percentage points in RP@5 on the HOUSE and TECH benchmark datasets
reported by <a class="paper-link" href="https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/11079977/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Decorte et al. (2025)</a>.
These benchmarks consist of job postings annotated with ESCO
gold-standard skill labels by human experts, against which model
predictions are evaluated. The reported RP@5 scores are 0.822 and 0.838,
respectively.
:::
:::

:::: {.section-card}
### AI Skills Demand

The demand for AI-related skills is highly dynamic, with new
technologies and tools emerging rapidly while others become obsolete.
This makes it difficult to track AI skill demand using static
taxonomies alone.

Lightcast captures highly granular and technology-specific signals, such
as individual AI tools, models, copilots, libraries, and chatbot
platforms. This makes it well suited to detecting early changes in
employer demand. The policy challenge is that these highly specific
skill labels do not automatically roll up into a small set of broader
categories that are easy to interpret and communicate.

To address this, we build on
<a class="paper-link" href="https://documents1.worldbank.org/curated/en/099451102172652767/pdf/IDU-e779b9af-e9b1-43ae-a2c5-9679dbf2d829.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Martins-Neto et al. (2026)</a>,
who organize Lightcast AI skills into a policy-friendly clustering
framework. Their approach groups the benchmark AI skill list into ten
clusters:

This clustering gives us a transparent and policy-relevant benchmark for
tracking AI demand. At the same time, the AI space is evolving quickly.
In the current Lightcast retrieval, we identify 114 additional AI
skills beyond the Martins-Neto benchmark, bringing the current total to
464 skills.

```{=html}
<div class="ai-cluster-grid">
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="AI Agent">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 1</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">AI Agent</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="AI Agent">29</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="AI Agent">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="AI Ethics, Governance and Regulations">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 2</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">AI Ethics, Governance and Regulations</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="AI Ethics, Governance and Regulations">8</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="AI Ethics, Governance and Regulations">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Artificial Intelligence">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 3</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Artificial Intelligence</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Artificial Intelligence">43</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Artificial Intelligence">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Autonomous Driving">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 4</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Autonomous Driving</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Autonomous Driving">14</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Autonomous Driving">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Generative AI">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 5</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Generative AI</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Generative AI">20</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Generative AI">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Machine Learning">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 6</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Machine Learning</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Machine Learning">105</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Machine Learning">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Natural Language Processing">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 7</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Natural Language Processing</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Natural Language Processing">59</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Natural Language Processing">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Neural Networks">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 8</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Neural Networks</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Neural Networks">29</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Neural Networks">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Robotics">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 9</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Robotics</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Robotics">15</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Robotics">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
  <button class="ai-cluster-card" type="button" data-ai-cluster="Visual Image Recognition">
    <span class="ai-cluster-index">Cluster 10</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-name">Visual Image Recognition</span>
    <span class="ai-cluster-count-group">
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-label">Skills n:</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-total" data-ai-cluster-total-count="Visual Image Recognition">28</span>
      <span class="ai-cluster-count-new">(+<span data-ai-cluster-new-count="Visual Image Recognition">0</span>)</span>
    </span>
  </button>
</div>
<div class="ai-cluster-legend">Values in parentheses indicate new additions to the Lightcast AI taxonomy.</div>
```

To make the expanded list more interpretable, we assign these 114 newer
Lightcast AI skills to the ten Martins-Neto clusters using an
embedding-based classification approach. This provides a practical way
to extend the benchmark taxonomy to newer tools and concepts, but these
additional assignments should still be treated as provisional and
subject to expert verification.

For that reason, we report two versions of AI demand below. The first is
a strict benchmark based on the 350 AI skills covered directly by the
Martins-Neto framework. The second uses the full current Lightcast AI
list of 464 skills, combining the original benchmark with 114 newly
retrieved skills that we classify into the same ten clusters using
embedding similarity.

```{=html}
<p class="notes source-note">Source: Lightcast AI skills taxonomy; Martins-Neto et al. (2026); authors' calculations.</p>
```
::::

## Results

::: {.section-card}
### Digital Skill Levels in Poland

Using the classification proposed by
<a class="paper-link" href="https://openknowledge.worldbank.org/entities/publication/b485a240-338e-4c86-8ade-3bf9c134ddce" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Angel-Urdinola, Chinen, and Rodon Casarramona (2025)</a>,
we group ESCO-linked digital skills into `basic`, `intermediate`, and
`advanced` categories. Across Poland, 696,092 digital skill mentions
from job postings can be classified in this way. The resulting profile
shows that advanced digital skills account for the largest share of
classified digital demand.

```{=html}
<div class="figure-caption-title">Figure 5. Distribution of classified digital skill mentions in Poland</div>
<div class="digital-stack-card">
  <div class="digital-stack-topline">
    <span class="digital-stack-total">696,092</span>
    <span class="digital-stack-subtext">classified digital skill mentions</span>
  </div>
  <div class="digital-stack-bar" aria-label="Stacked distribution of digital skill levels in Poland">
    <div class="digital-stack-segment digital-stack-advanced" style="width:43.45%">
      <span>43.45%</span>
    </div>
    <div class="digital-stack-segment digital-stack-basic" style="width:33.13%">
      <span>33.13%</span>
    </div>
    <div class="digital-stack-segment digital-stack-intermediate" style="width:23.42%">
      <span>23.42%</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="digital-stack-legend">
    <div class="digital-stack-legend-item">
      <span class="digital-stack-dot digital-stack-advanced"></span>
      <span class="digital-stack-name">Advanced</span>
      <span class="digital-stack-share">43.45%</span>
    </div>
    <div class="digital-stack-legend-item">
      <span class="digital-stack-dot digital-stack-basic"></span>
      <span class="digital-stack-name">Basic</span>
      <span class="digital-stack-share">33.13%</span>
    </div>
    <div class="digital-stack-legend-item">
      <span class="digital-stack-dot digital-stack-intermediate"></span>
      <span class="digital-stack-name">Intermediate</span>
      <span class="digital-stack-share">23.42%</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
<p class="notes source-note">Source: Angel-Urdinola, D., Chinen, M. H., and Rodon Casarramona, G. 2025. <em>Digital Skills Development: Competence Frameworks, Assessment Tools, and Pedagogical Approaches</em>. Education Notes, 203091; Pracuj.pl job postings; authors' mapping of classified ESCO-linked digital skill mentions.</p>
```
:::

::: {.section-card}
### AI Demand Snapshot

```{=html}
<div class="ai-stats-grid">
  <article class="ai-stat-card">
    <p class="ai-stat-label">Strict Martins-Neto benchmark</p>
    <p class="ai-stat-value">0.67%</p>
    <p class="ai-stat-subtext">4,997 of 749,569 postings are identified as AI-related under a conservative specification based on the 350 benchmark skills.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="ai-stat-card">
    <p class="ai-stat-label">Expanded current Lightcast list</p>
    <p class="ai-stat-value">1.02%</p>
    <p class="ai-stat-subtext">7,669 of 749,569 postings are identified as AI-related when the 114 additional Lightcast AI skills are also included.</p>
  </article>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="figure-caption-title">Figure 4. Most demanded Lightcast AI clusters and skills in Poland</div>
<div id="ai-cluster-demand"></div>
<p class="notes source-note">Source: Pracuj.pl job postings; Lightcast AI skills taxonomy; Martins-Neto et al. (2026); authors' semantic matching and aggregation. Cluster demand is based on postings matched to the expanded Lightcast AI list under the conservative threshold used in this report.</p>
```
:::

:::: {.section-card}
### Lightcast AI Skills Explorer

The table below lists the retrieved Lightcast AI skills used in this
report. It distinguishes between the Martins-Neto benchmark skills and
newer Lightcast AI skills that have appeared beyond that benchmark. For
the newer skills, the cluster shown here comes from our embedding-based
classification and should therefore be treated as provisional pending
expert verification. Use the search box or filters to narrow the list,
or click a cluster card above to jump directly to that cluster. Within a
cluster view, benchmark skills are shown first, followed by our newly
classified skills in a different color. Click `Description` to open the full
Lightcast description in a modal window.

```{=html}
<div id="lightcast-ai-skills-table"></div>
<div id="lightcast-ai-skill-modal" class="skill-modal hidden" aria-hidden="true">
  <div class="skill-modal-backdrop" data-close-modal="true"></div>
  <div class="skill-modal-dialog" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="skill-modal-title">
    <button class="skill-modal-close" type="button" aria-label="Close" data-close-modal="true">x</button>
    <p class="skill-modal-kicker">Lightcast AI skill</p>
    <h3 id="skill-modal-title"></h3>
    <div class="skill-modal-meta" id="skill-modal-meta"></div>
    <div class="skill-modal-body" id="skill-modal-body"></div>
  </div>
</div>
```
::::

::::: {.section-card .map-shell}
### Regional AI Demand

The map below shows AI demand intensity by voivodeship, measured as the
number of postings matched to at least one skill from the expanded
Lightcast AI list at the conservative similarity threshold used in this
report, normalized by the 2025 labour force. Click a voivodeship to
inspect its three most characteristic Lightcast AI skills. To identify
which skills are particularly characteristic of individual
voivodeships, we apply the log-likelihood ratio (`G²`) test following
Dunning (1993), comparing each region with the rest of the country.

```{=html}
<div class="figure-caption-title" id="ai-map-figure-title"></div>
<div class="ai-regional-layout">
  <div id="ai-regional-map"></div>
  <div id="ai-regional-detail"></div>
</div>
<div class="map-caption source-note" id="ai-map-note"></div>
```
::::

## References

:::: {.section-card}
```{=html}
<div class="reference-list">
  <p>Angel-Urdinola, D., Chinen, M. H., and Rodon Casarramona, G. 2025. <em>Digital Skills Development: Competence Frameworks, Assessment Tools, and Pedagogical Approaches</em>. Education Notes, 203091.</p>
  <p>Decorte, J. J., Van Hautte, J., Develder, C., and Demeester, T. 2025. <em>Efficient Text Encoders for Labor Market Analysis</em>. IEEE Access.</p>
  <p>Dunning, T. 1993. <em>Accurate Methods for the Statistics of Surprise and Coincidence</em>. Computational Linguistics 19(1): 61-74.</p>
  <p>Martins-Neto, A., Liu, Y., Khurana, S., and Porras Lopez, J. M. 2026. <em>Click, Code, Earn: The Returns to Digital Skills</em>. World Bank.</p>
</div>
```
::::

```{=html}
<script src="assets/plotly.min.js"></script>
<script src="assets/regional_map_data.js"></script>
<script src="assets/ai_regional_map_data.js"></script>
<script src="assets/lightcast_ai_skills_data.js"></script>
<script src="assets/ai_report.js"></script>
<script src="assets/lightcast_ai_table.js"></script>
<script src="assets/report.js"></script>
```
